Hi, I’m a budtender in Alaska and my shop sells both THC only edibles as well as THC:CBD 1:1 edibles. Basically, for just the psychoactive effects with the mild physical effects of the flower the THC only edibles are best. Indica edibles are good for sleep, even without CBD, and Sativa edibles are good for socializing and anxiety (in appropriate doses; high dose sativa anything may worsen anxiety.) Either way, with only THC the most effect a person will get is a potent head/body high. The 1:1 THC/CBD edibles are best for helping medicinally. A lot of veterans and older people with aches, pains, illnesses or injuries come specifically for those. The true effects of pain relief, sleep aid, anti anxiety, etc come from the combination of THC and CBD, so while the high might not be as potent as just THC, it is a well-rounded, longer lasting head/body high that is more medically beneficial than THC only edibles. If you’ve never tried 1:1 edibles before, I’d recommend trying them just to have an idea of the difference. You might find you prefer 1:1 over just THC, maybe the other way around. Either way, enjoy your weed and happy smoking!

Electric vapes are easy to clean. I LOVED my Arizer Argo when I used it frequently. The glass stems have a "pre-portion" chamber which you can purchase a cap to halve the portion if you so desire. The stems click in and out super easy, and to clean just soak them for a little while in isopropyl and rinse with warm water. Occasionally take a Q tip with a slight dash of isopropyl and squeeze it out so there's absolutely 0 drippage and swab out any debris in the bottom where you insert the glass stem. I used mine religiously for about 2 years and needed to swab it maybe 2 or 3 times and I used that bad boy daily at least twice. As for use if you pack your portion full do not pack it tight, set the temp to 420F and let it "cook" for about 2-3 minutes and slowly sip on it. One sesh with 1 stem pack can last 10-15 minutes. The key is to set the right temp and SLOWLY SIP. It works off of contact heat and convection heat, so if you pull too hard you'll just cool it off and butcher your hits. Slow sips also help me relax too, just the mentality of taking it slow y'know? Hope this was helpful!

Your answer feels like you my comment (anyway gotta say I could phrase my point way better). The key takeaway of that article is that highest percentage doesn't have to mean the "best weed" (while term "best weed" is highly subjective). I do not doubt the science (as I, in fact, agree with that point), and also I do not say that alleged 2% difference is recognizable (it was definitely some Karen lol). I'm also well aware that the THC percentage numbers when buying seeds are kinda informative and that the finished product can have different THC levels depending on many factors during the growth (but at the same time you definitely won't get 20+% from seed advertised as 1%). What I wanted to say that percentages aren't completely useless - while it says nothing about the tastiness of the bud, structure, kind of high etc, it's an informative value suggesting how hard it will hit when you smoke it. I especially felt it after last tolerance break, after which I could easily go whole day smoking 1% strains while 3 puffs of my strongest stuff would get me absolutely stoned. (Also sometimes I did an involuntarily blind test when I accidentally lighted up a wrong joint thinking it's the light stuff only to be sent to outer space a few moments later lol). Anyways, yep - it feels stupid to buy weed based on the percentage alone and just going for the highest numbers, unless you have a clear desire just to get fucked up as much as you can. I personally prefer different strains for different occasions. When I'm working from home, it's mostly CBD weed with very low THC levels for me, just to feel a little tickle and satisfy the craving. When in the club, I stick to some "medium" stuff so I feel it a bit more while still keeping my social skills. And when I'm at home and it's after 11PM and all I gotta do before bed is just to relax playing some PC game or work on my music, why the hell not to enjoy the strongest shit. I’m speaking of the general mass that takes edibles from the ones that .5mg can get them a nice buzz to the people like me that can eat 4g of RSO and still not have a good time with it. THC is converted to 11-oh-THC through the metabolic process by the Cytochrome P2C9 enzyme. Some people just break down THC too fast to really have it convert over 11-oh-THC, and is referred to as “the bummer gene”, which performing copy you get depends on genetic variability. Some people literally just can’t get high off eating THC and it has nothing to do with tolerance. Some people will never get past not just getting absolutely ripped off super small amounts. Now the fun thing about all of this is that actual tincture(THC+solvent, not THC+carrier) will deliver THC through your lymphatic system that crosses the blood brain barrier incredibly fast and completely bypasses your first pass metabolism. Mixing Everclear and decarb’d cannabis using a quick heat extraction of THC from plant into ethanol and you have some really strong shit. So if you don’t mind drinking small(1-5mL) of Everclear? Then tincture is for you. Edit: I re-read what you said and I’d always recommend RSO or homemade edibles to you. That’ll be the cheapest way you could consume edibles that would work for you. I take smoking bud, not trim, no BS, good weed. I grind it up, maybe a half ounce at a time. I make a tinfoil envelope put the weed in and seal it tight by crimping the edges.I then wrap that in tin foil. I pre-heat the oven to 230-250F and decarb for a half hour to an hour with the envelop flat in the center of the oven. I remove the envelope from the oven and allow it too cool. I put the decarbed weed into a mason jar and cover it with 195 proof everclear, only a smidge more liquid than there is flower. If you are working with a small quantity like an 8th, use one of those wedding favor snap top glass jars(remove the rubber gasket) or a similar thin glass vessel with a lid so the alcohol to weed ratio is not dilute. I swirl it for maybe a minute maybe less and store it in a cool dark place safe from heat. Every 6 hours or so I swirl it around (not when I'm sleeping I just let it sit lol). After 24 hours I filter it through cheese cloth. I then squeeze the remaining cheese cloth with a handheld orange juicer/press thing for making orange juice. This gets mostly everything out. I then bottle up a dripper bottle, and store the rest in the freezer with a nice label. Should be close to 20-30 mL of tincture per 8th. This is full spectrum shit, practically rocket fuel for me. Think RSO, not shitty distillate tincture from dispensaries. 1mL is about \ 25-50mg(depending on the starting material) you honestly don't want to take that dose even if you're used to "50mg edibles". I recommend starting with 3-7 drops, and working your way up. Dripper bottles that have volume graduations are very handy here If that isn't concentrated enough I have tips, that kinda ruin the flavor a bit, but yea. I have some secrets to maintaining terpene profiles (to some extent), but I'm not ready to share those yet.

Cannabis, a plant with a rich history of medicinal and recreational use, contains various chemical compounds that offer potential therapeutic benefits. However, when cannabis is ignited and smoked in a joint or blunt, the heat and combustion process can lead to the destruction or alteration of certain beneficial components. In this essay, we will explore the potential degradation of beneficial parts of cannabis when it is lit up in a joint or blunt. Terpenes are aromatic compounds found in cannabis that not only contribute to its distinct scent and flavor but also possess potential therapeutic properties. However, when cannabis is lit up, the high temperatures can cause the degradation or evaporation of these volatile compounds. This loss of terpenes not only impacts the overall sensory experience but also potentially reduces the entourage effect—the synergistic interaction between cannabinoids and terpenes that may enhance therapeutic benefits. The combustion process involved in smoking cannabis can lead to the formation of harmful byproducts. When cannabis burns, it produces toxins such as tar, carbon monoxide, and other carcinogenic compounds found in tobacco smoke. These substances can have detrimental effects on the respiratory system, increasing the risk of respiratory issues and long-term health problems.Smoking cannabis in a joint or blunt may not be the most efficient method of consumption. When cannabis is burned, much of the active compounds are lost through smoke that is either not inhaled or wasted between puffs. This inefficient delivery system reduces the bioavailability of cannabinoids and other beneficial components, making it less effective compared to alternative consumption methods like vaporization or edibles.To maximize the therapeutic potential of cannabis, alternative consumption methods can be considered. Vaporization, for instance, heats cannabis at lower temperatures, preserving more cannabinoids and terpenes while minimizing the production of harmful byproducts. Edibles, tinctures, and topicals provide another option for consuming cannabis without subjecting it to combustionWhile cannabis consumption through smoking joints or blunts remains popular, it is important to recognize the potential drawbacks associated with this method. The high temperatures and combustion process can result in the destruction or alteration of beneficial components, including cannabinoids and terpenes, while introducing harmful byproducts. Exploring alternative consumption methods that prioritize the preservation of these valuable compounds can help individuals harness the full potential of cannabis while minimizing potential risks to their health.

That brand offers their capsules made with either RSO or Distillate. This is the Distillate version. So, the oil used to make it has been distilled, like you'd distill water, or, say vodka. That process purifies the oil in a sense, so you'll be left with primarily pure cannabinoids. The effects tend to come on strong and are often described as a "clean high" (think: comparing a triple distilled vodka shot to drinking a dark beer). The dark beer here is RSO. It is raw, mostly unprocessed cannabis oil, so it has more plant in it. You'll usually hear that called "full spectrum" which basically means that it has most of the terpines in tact along with other plant material. That adds more depth to the effects. If you're using it medicinally, you TYPICALLY want RSO, as it offers more of what the strain has to offer. Some people just prefer the distillate, even as medicine because it gives them the reliving feeling they like more. I'd recommend trying one version for a few days then the other to see what you think works best for you.

Vape Pen Vape pens are a healthy and efficient way to dial in your THC Oil dosage. Be sure to source your vape pens from a reputable source. Black-market vapes can cause serious health concerns. Assumption 1: A vape pen contains 0.5g of THC Oil Assumption 2: The THC Oil is at 80% Concentration (400mg THC) Assumption 3: 100 puffs from a 0.5g vape pen Conclusion: 1 puff = 4mg THC Bowls / Pipes If you are looking for a sizeable amount of THC in a single puff, dry-herb / flower might be one of your best bets. THC dosage for Bowls and Pipes are given as follows: Assumption 1: A bowl contains 0.3g of flower Assumption 2: 20% THC concentration (60mg THC) Assumption 3: 5 puffs per bowl Conclusion: 1 puff = 12mg THC Joint / Blunt Joints and blunts allow for a smaller THC per puff dosage, but typically burn longer and would allow for multiple hits, shared amongst fellow consumers. Assumption 1: A blunt / joint contains 0.5g of flower Assumption 2: 20% THC Concentration (100mg THC) Assumption 3: 30 puffs from a 0.5g blunt / joint. Conclusion: 1 puff = 3.33mg THC Bong While consuming THC from a bong might not be the most common approach, it is certainly an effective way to get a maximal dose. Assumption 1: A bong’s bowl contains 0.3g of flower Assumption 2: 20% THC Concentration (60mg THC) Assumption 3: 3 hits from a bong bowl Conclusion: 1 bong hit = 30mg THC Dabs / Concentrate Dabbing THC is a clean and effective way to get a sizeable dose. Be sure to follow best dabbing temperature guidance to make sure you don’t lose the other terpenes and trace cannabinoids. Assumption 1: The average dab container contains 0.5g of concentrate Assumption 2: The dab is at 80% THC Concentration (400mg THC) Assumption 3: An average-sized dab (size of a grain of rice) is 25mg (1/20th of the container) Conclusion: 1 dab = 20mg THC Vaporizer (Dry Flower) Vaping Dry Flower THC is a bit easier to calculate, since you have more control over temperature. Popular vaping products like the Pax fall into this dosage guidance category. Assumption 1: A vaporizer contains 0.3g of flower Assumption 2: 20% THC Concentration (60mg THC) Assumption 3: 6 puffs from a vaporizer with 0.3g of flower Conclusion: 1 vape hit = 10mg THC Many people also ask “do you hold in THC vape?” after using our THC dosage calculator. The answer is simple: Nope! Both THC and CBD are absorbed in your lungs almost immediately upon inhalation and contact with your alveoli.

dispensary edibles in canada are not legally permitted to contain over 10mg per package. you are not getting 100mg for $18, you are getting 10mg. each of the ten gummies will contain 1mg. unless you are buying a black market product from an unlicensed shop, it’s physically impossible to have 10 10mg gummies in one package. pretty much every single package of edibles has 10mg total thc(not cbd ones) in every canadian dispensary. this law is to prevent children from becoming overly intoxicated upon accidental ingestion. 10mg is semi-manageable for a child, 100mg is not. every package that is THC edibles is gonna be either 5mg or 10mg THC. just buy the cheapest package if you’re concerned about price, and yes the different edibles will have slightly different highs, they are not all made from the same strain or company or batch. it’s very likely that some of the pricier edibles will have higher quality concentrates in them

Today's review is a great strain from Apollo called Gumbo. It's got a great spacey and euphoric feel to it while still being very relaxing and indica leaning and smells and tastes pretty good to boot. Let's get into the full review. Packaging - The flower came in a small glass jar that was actually meant for gummies since they ran out of jars with the right label, but they did label the lid with a sticker to let me know. I don't really care what the label looks like personally, I just appreciate that it came in a glass jar, which I always prefer over a plastic jar or bag. The jar fits the bud well and is something I can reuse for other bud in the future as well. Appearance/Feel - My 3.5g weighed out to 4.4g, so almost a whole gram overweight, which is pretty awesome. The buds are very pretty and are a decent size. My 4.4g consisted of 6 buds. The trim job is pretty good. There are a few stems that could have been cropped a little shorter, but not bad especially considering that it was so much overweight. They are a beautiful mix of colors with some greens, plenty of dark purples, some oranges, and a very frosty covering of trichomes over everything giving it a fuzzy white look as well. The buds are fairly dense and seem to have a good cure. On first inspection it felt a little dry, but after breaking some buds apart and grinding it up it has more moisture than I thought. They snap nicely when breaking apart and are fairly sticky when grinding up and made a nice fluffy grind with a good moisture content that worked well for smoking and vaping. Smell - The smell on this one is quite nice provided you like that good ol funk. It's pretty strong and has a little fruit to it, but leans more earthy and skunky with a little pine and almost a sour hint to it. It's pretty savory and pungent and fills the room pretty quickly once you pop open that jar. Taste - I tried this through my dry herb vaporizer (Airvape Legacy Pro) and also rolled in a joint. When vaped the flavor came through really well. It was similar to the smell and pretty strong. I got plenty of vapor throughout a 5 minute session that was thick and smooth. When smoked it was nice and smooth and almost creamy tasting. It had some of that savory funk, a touch of sour, and just a little fruit in there. It smoked well with very little coughing and the ash was mostly white. Effects - I don't know the THC percentage on this one, but whatever it is the effects are strong. It smacked me around harder than I thought it would. The first time I tried it was a decent sized joint and by the time I was halfway through with it I was pretty well on the moon. It felt just as potent vaped too. It has a very nice euphoric feel that's pretty spacey and tingly. One of those strains you smoke and when it hits you you just say, "Damn that's good!". I did get some pretty intense dry mouth with it and the munchies hit pretty hard. It definitely enhanced touch sensations, the shower felt extra hot and times with the wifey felt extra good if you're picking up what I'm putting down. I didn't get any racing thoughts or heart or any real paranoia or anxiety I sometimes get from strong strains (sativa in particular), and it was relaxing and ended up making me feel pretty drowsy. Time did feel like it slowed down some too. Effects lasted a good hour and a half to two hours I'd say and I slept really well. Pretty top notch effects in my opinion, right in line with what I love about cannabis. Conclusion - This turned out to be a pretty awesome strain that has just about everything I like. The coloration is beautiful and it's super frosty. The cure was pretty spot on. The smell and taste is strong with a classic savory funk to it. The effects are relaxing while still being very strong, tingly, and euphoric. Just make sure you are ready for some dry mouth, munchies, and to feel pretty sedated.

Hey there sorry you had a bad experience, so lemme shed a little light on for you for next time, so CBD alone (Isolate and broad spectrum) shouldn't ever give you any psychoactive effects that make you feel anxious by itself. I always always recommend people visit a well-established dispensary to buy any CBD or Delta variant products as the employees can help guide you to where you need to be and what you're looking to get out of CBD rather than buying something from a gas station and having no information given to you + shoddy brands (Looking at you, Cake) (x2 by no means are all CBD or delta brands sold at gas stations bad, just do your research and err on the side of caution) Delta 9 is going to be the main variant of THC that you find in any strain of marijuana, and in laymans terms Delta 8 is going to be about 50% less psychoactive than delta 9. Delta 10 I can't exactly speak on, as we don't carry that in our store, but the gist that I gather from vendors that want to work with us say that it acts like a sativa (uplifting, more active, but on the flipside can induce anxiety as well) Many gummies are mixed with both CBD and one of the deltas to help with the entourage effect which basically means shared properties of CBD and THC work together to help your ailments just a little bit better than an isolate. My take? If you're new to CBD gummies mixed with any THC, go low, go slow. You can always take more, but you can't take it back. Cut that single gummy in half, or even a quarter and see how it affects you, give it about three days or wherever your comfortable, and take a little more if you feel like you need a bit more potency. This all is coming from more of a health and beneficial side, rather than a simply looking to get high side. What you're looking for is a good mix between THC percentage and terpenes. I'm going to go over some common misconceptions that people talk about all the time but are false 1. Higher THC means better Higher THC doesn't necessarily mean better, In order for marijuana to work THC needs to bind with your CBD and THC receptors, but your high is also heavily influenced by the terpenes that are in the plant. A good similar way of looking at this is aromatherapy on a higher more effective scale. More THC percentage doesn't always mean more potent, look for Bud or concentrate that is testing in the 3-5% range in terpenes, you'll notice their THC amounts are significantly lower but they will still kick your ass 2. Purple means it's really good. The amount or color of purple on the bud does not translate to quality. Most people don't know this but growing bud in a colder climate gives it this purple color. This has nothing to do with THC on the surface level. 3. Pricey means worth it I recently went to a dispensary where they were charging $35 an eighth for some white runtz and it was dry and harsher than an 80's alcoholic step dad. And most dispensaries you are allowed to look smell and sometimes touch which you are buying b courteous of how clean your hands are, try to waft instead of holding your entire face over the jar, and most importantly if they don't let you see it before you buy it, DO NOT shop there. MY FINAL CONSENSUS IS do your own research, look at Bud very closely and carefully determine if the terpene percentage is what you want, also you can determine how good the terpenes are with the smell. looking at the terpene chart is also a good idea of knowing what your bud is going to do to you, I recommend going to dispensaries with the most information readily available to you, that usually means that they grow in a more professional way and they are more organized. I've walked into dispensaries that have charged out the ass for bud that you can see growing from the lobby, I've gotten home broken it up and it's falling apart in my hands like dust, I've had concentrates be the wrong consistency, and I've had stuff not hit me when it's testing 28. Just do your research.

Yes, you will experience different highs based on method of consumption. Smoking give you an immediate high, and vaping does as well, but vaping produces a much cleaner "high". This is due to the fact that you are not combusting (lighting aflame) the flower, rather you are heating it and inhaling the pure vapors- there's no smoke involved. In the case of vaping- whether it is liquid or dry herb- because there's no smoke and it is only pure vapor, many tend tonexperience not only a cleaner high, but a more intense one. As for edibles, yes, it does have a different effect because instead of giving an immediate high as with smoking or vaping, you won't feel any effects for at least 30 minutes to up to 2 hours because your body has to digest the product first before any THC gets into your bloodstream. It is a much slower process but produces one of the most powerful highs. If you've never tried edibles before and want to, be warned: start low and slow. Everybody (including myself) has the same experience: "I tried some and didn't feel anything after a few minutes so I tried more. Still didn't feel high so I ate more. Still nothing so I tried more... but then it hit after about an hour and I fel like I was going crazy/going to die". Peoples experiences all tend to be some variation on this theme. They took too much and then felt crappy. Now, we all know weed can't kill you, so you aren't going to die, but it can make you incredibly uncomfortable for several hours, maybe even a couple days depending on your experience level, weight, etc. So always, always, always, whe doing edibles, don't just chow down on an entire brownie or bag of gummies. Break off little pieces, then give it an hour and see how you feel. If you feel nothing, try a little more. But just remember, this is going to produce a different high vs smoking or vaping because it's going to enter your bloodstream vs going straight to your brain. All in all, just be careful. If you do accidentally have too much edibles, try to counteract with some CBD and that may help you recover a little fast, but ultimately you just have to wait it out. It'll eventually work its way out of your system. Have fun!

It’s all about moderation and the plants make up. Marijuana needs to be further studied. To dig deeper, check out how terpenoids and terpenes affect the make up of plant strains, sativa and indica. Which most people generalize the two into categories but the specific bud names matter if you can test the plants make up. To quickly summarize terpenes are the genetic make up of a plant; what makes the plant smell a certain way, look a certain color, like purple, light green, or orange “hairs”, and how they feel sticky like super glue, or mids with no crystals under a light. Also the fresher the buds the more potent the effects the terpenes will have. That why people keep there stuff sealed. It’s kind of similar to when potato chips go stale after a day if the bags left open and don’t taste as good or when medications are expired yet still effective, just not as potent. Terpenes create the chemical effects your body produces too. If you smoke a plant with a specific type of terpene(s), theyre the reason you feel happy, calm, relaxed, hysterical, energized, even anxious, etc. It depends on the terpene that’s in the plants compound but you would have to test the plants to find out, which is why most people go off strain names then label name. For example Sativas; super sour is made up of different terpenes than sativa: gorilla glue or indica: grand daddy purple vs indica blue dream (which is known also in hybrid terms)… but I hope you get the idea lol bc their known to contain specific terpenes which produce their specific name sourced from the category of sativa/indica. Another reason why I only consume from trusted sources is bc they can provide these details and dispensaries do too, which is why they’re considered higher quality. You can sometimes find this info on labels. You can also determine this yourself partially, using your own senses, upon inspection aka how sticky it is, how loud it smells, what it smells like such as pinesol or sweet like candy kush, and by inspecting the buds. Or try out samples and get quantity in what you like before it’s gone lol. Don’t just go off of basic strain names like sativa and indica, it’s so much more complex that that. To dig into this a little for you, Do all edibles have the same high, nope absolutely not but based off what your describing you probably have distillate edibles, this holds true for cartridges as well Types of edibles: Distilate Full spectrum Cannabinoid blends Distillate: This is just thc isolated out of the source plant, many distilate gummies won't declare themselves sativa or indica because when you take just the THC you leave behind the other cannabinoids and terpenes, just thc isolated from an indica is about the same as just THC isolated from a sativa, so they'll say sativa or indica but you're taking the same thing and they're misleading in that way. If you wanted to get strain effects or like an indica or sativa effect you need full spectrum and not distilate. The pro of distilate is that it's cheap, the con is that the high isn't as nice and they're all the same effect wise and a lot less medicinal Full Spectrum: These guys have the THC from distilate and then also the other cannabinoids and terpenes from the cannabis plant, when you smoke bud those are the three parts of the plant that give you your specific type of high (check out kaleidoscope effect or entourage effect) full spectrum gummies have all three parts so they feel more like flower and you will see indica vs sativa differences. Cannabinoid blends: You can get these with either distilate or full spectrum but your essentially taking the product above and adding a bunch of another cannabinoid most commonly CBD but you can find blends with CBN for sleep aids and some other rarer cannabinoids (wyld has CBG gummies for example)

You've experienced what everyone who uses weed at for some time has- greenout. No, you've not broken your weed usage. You went too hard or got the wrong strain. Or both. Stick to the lowest dosage that gives you the feeling you want. Build up to it slowly. If you don't know the effect a strain has on you, start REAL low and build up. Try and stick with the same usage type- pen, vape, blunt whatever- so that you can figure out how each affects you. Blunts are harsh and fast, vapes are less harsh and slower etc. If you want to experiment, do so in a safe place, either alone or with people you trust, so if you do greenout you have support or don't get even more triggered by either being alone or with people who will freak you out. Most of all- weed is a drug. Like all drugs, your body has a safe dosage. With weed, that dose changes over time due to tolerance and won't kill you if you 'overdose' but you'll have a REAL bad time if you go over it and it's the wrong strain/situation/mood. Weed amplifies mood, so if you smoke feeling anxious, it'll make it worse. This comes from someone who uses weed to treat anxiety- yes, it makes it worse if you mis-dose. Same with depression. It can help, but get the dose wrong and it'll make it worse. So just don't go nuts and stick with strains that make you feel how you want.

I'd definitely recommend a little flower, an AIO disposable vape, and some gummies (or RSO). I'd go for a good mid or a topshelf flower on BOGO. The bang for your buck falls off pretty rapidly towards the upper end. Unless your a connoisseur your likely not gonna notice the difference between the absolute top tier and a step or two down. With vapes your either gonna get distillate or live resin/rosin. Distillate is the entry level product. It's gonna be the cheapest. From there you have live resin. It's got more cannabinoids and terpenes. Distillate is straight THC while live resin has more of the plant. This makes the effects from smoking it a little more like flower. Some people describe the high from distillate as "one dimensional". Live rosin is the top tier vape product. It's usually beyond my price range. I find the live resin is a good middle ground. It can usually be had for not much more than distillate. Most your gummies are fairly similar. They are going to be made with distillate. You can buy live resin gummies. I've never noticed a huge difference. What I would recommend is buying a syringe of RSO. In my state you can get 100 mg of gummies for $20 or 800mg of RSO for $20. Just think of rso as the gummy without the candy. You just squirt a small bit on peanut butter.

You mention in another comment that the product you're using is hemp. All hemp flower contains some amount of THC/THCa. THCa is converted to THC when decarboxylated (smoked, etc.). THC is well known to cause panic attacks for some, so the culprit here is almost certainly THC. Stick with broad-spectrum or isolate in the future, and avoid hemp flower. I have nothing against hemp flower and use it often myself, but it is without a doubt, generally speaking, the most intoxicating method of using CBD due to the THC content, which is almost always higher than traditional full-spectrum products like infused oil, distillate vape cartridges, etc.

THCV cannot break down into THC, it is two carbons shorter. The pentyl chain on THC is from its precursor, CBGA, which in turn gets it from it precursor, olivetolic acid. You'd have to add an ethyl to the end of THCV to make THC, and most drug metabolism in the body is taken care of by monooxygenases from the liver (cytochrome P450's) that just add -OH groups to something until it's water soluble enough to get flushed out by the kidneys. However, THCV and the other -varins get a propyl group instead of a pentyl group if there are shorter precursors present, with propyl groups coming off the aromatic ring. In the plant, these shorter precursors go through the same enzymes as their longer-chain counterparts would (like CBGA/ THCA/ CBDA/ CBCA synthases), and get folded in certain ways that leave the hydrocarbon chain exposed as a sort of tail. The cannabivarins interact with the receptors in a partially antagonistic way, because the pharmacophore of phytocannabinoids is that hydrocarbon chain coming off of that aromatic group. The lipophilic sidechain leading up to an aromatic ring with high electron density in the form of conjugated pi-orbitals closely mimics the electronic signature of the fatty acid amides such as anadamide (one of the endocannabinoid ligands for the CB1 receptor). So the longer than chain is, such as dimethylheptylpyran or tetrahydrocannabiphorol, the more agonism at the CB1 receptor you will observe, and inversely, the more truncated this sidechain length is, the more anatagonistic the receptor activity can get. In short, the more the cannabinoid resembles an omega-6 fatty acid, the more it resembles the endogenous ligand the receptor evolved to accept (which is why 5 carbons up to a double bonded 6th carbon is the magical spot for THC & CBD). Many of our receptors require a nitrogen somewhere in the structure for a drug or neurotransmitter to be active and that's what makes cannabinoids so unique in nature, they're not water soluble as well due to the lack of a free base (nitrogen's lone pair). You are stacking a lot of phytocannabinoids on top of each other so the interactions will be complex. I think your second assessment is accurate, that at a higher concentration THCV becomes a CB1R agonist, you have a lot of other CB1R agonists and allosteric modulators in that tincture of yours and that is the most abundant G-coupled protein receptor in the brain. There's plenty of them to bind to, and I would be willing to bet that 25 mg of THCV in a tincture by itself would not send you over the edge the same way that the entourage cocktail would. Perhaps titrate back to the 5-10 mg range for now.

Cannabis is very complex, so I don’t blame you. When you are choosing a CBD product (from industrial hemp), you have 3 options to choose from; Full Spectrum, Broad Spectrum, & Isolate. Full Spectrum is literally the entire hemp plant (CBD, CBG, CBN, CBC) but made specifically to have less than 0.3% delta-9 THC by dry weight. Broad Spectrum is just like Full, but just with undetectable levels of delta-9 THC. Isolate CBD is purified even more to just have CBD & nothing else. The gummies you have are full spectrum due to the delta-9 THC. Each gummy has 50mg of CBD & 5mg of THC, or in other words, a 10:1 CBD:THC ratio. Usually for beginners, it’s best to start with broad spectrum to test how you would react before jumping into THC, but it looks like you can handle it quite well. Regarding the drug test, you will most certainly test positive for delta-9 THC since you’re using full spectrum & even with broad spectrum; there’s no promises. I also advise against operating any vehicles while under the influence of cannabis since it alters your mood & thought process. Keep in mind that the more CBD there is, the less of the THC you’ll feel since CBD negates the effects of THC. Your CB1 & CB2 receptors in your brain are responsible on how you feel. Let me know if you have any other questions!

This stuff normally looks like your average sticky wax, kind of resembles Candle wax what color and appearance can definitely change. With this stuff you typically grab a small glob, superheat (to a certain temp range) a glass bucket called a "banger or nail" (I use a terp slurper) that you put in your bong. Once the wax touches the hot glass it should vaporize on contact and you smoke it. There's also attachments that allow you to smoke it out of a pen or electronically without using a torch 3. Cartridges aka pens, dab pen, wax pen, cart, etc... Arguably your most common and concealable method of consumption, it is essentially wax in a syrupy viscosity which is then put into the cartridge with a small coil in it. Once you press the button or activate the battery that it's attached to it powers the coil, the coil heats and vaporizes the wax. 4. Edibles The most concealable but arguably the heaviest hitting. Edibles are foods that have THC mixed in with them, And usually take about 15 to 45 minutes to take effect. 100 mg is usually the amount you buy for one package of gummies or cookies. For a first time user 100 mg will knock you on your ass. Start slowly with 20 to 40 and experiment with how much gets you to where you need to be, be extremely careful with edibles because they tend to get people really bad experiences and can be overpowering. I'm not going to explain to you the dangers of buying black market weed as it is commonly known, but I will tell you that having a peace of mind of having test results, no pesticides, no heavy metals, and state regulations really do quell some worries that I had when buying Black market. There were times where I was worried that something I got was laced (probably wasn't Im just paranoid) but couldn't be 100% for sure. Happy smoking Hope this helped!

Oregon here: you can get 36 buck oz of 30%+ THC weed here but it's gonna smoke hard even if it smells good - it's not done by potency but by quality here. Now, those nugs make really good edibles, which is what I hunt for - good strains with nice aromas of high potency but hard smoking so they get shoved into bargain bin for sometimes a buck a g. You can pay 400 all in for an oz that's only 15% THC (or even less) but is farmer's stash grade smoke - burns to almost nothing, tastes sweet, doesn't burn hot or oily, the bouquet has had time to ripen and open up, hasn't been beat up in transit, etc. It's the handling and the quality, not the strength or the quantity that determines what you'll pay here. It varies a lot but that's just because we continue to have what OLCC calls a "surplus" (what we call their mismanagement but that's not the question asked) so sometimes you can get deals that low of mid to top shelf grade flower (still not farmer's reserve but you gotta know people for that) here and there but you gotta hop or they get bought up. I mean, I don't have to post it here, Oregon dispensaries are pretty diligent about putting all their menus and info online, prices, potency, etc. Go look at some place like Floyd's or Gnome Grown menu on Leafly (not affiliated with any of them, I just don't know why people ask here when the dispensary menus are online). If you have a medical card and set up a provider you can have pounds of farmer's reserve grade material for almost nothing and the dispensaries give massive discounts on bulk deals to card holders at all grades and potencies of material. Oregon also reciprocates other State's medical program cards, so if you're a card holder in your state you can avail yourselves of what benefits we do have.

I tried to explain this to a friend the other day before he bought his pax. Pax is kind of like the apple brand of the vape world. It does what it does well and is fairly reliable but overall the technology used is really cheap and found on $50 vapes and it rarely changes. Similar to an iPhone. I use an iPhone but realistically my iPhone 11s is no different than my buddies iPhone 14. Just like my iPhone 11 was almost no different from my 8. They just don’t change or really do anything new with the technology like other brands. They just release them because people buy them. Pax is like that. They became a big name and are sold everywhere. People unknowingly claim they are the best. Realistically they use full conduction which is what all the cheap vapes use. It just comes in a nice sleek package and looks good. It’s not worth $200. Not to say conduction is awful because it has its place but realistically it’s a cheaper technology that’s done even better with a butane vape. The mighty is a far better product using hybrid heating but relies a lot on convection. Something like the tinymight 2 uses full convection and can heat up on 3-5s allowing on demand hits. So to me that kind of technology is worth $350. A pax is basically just an overpriced vape that you could get similar quality out of for under $75. For $75 you can start getting hybrid heating that works a lot better. If you like big milky clouds with subpar taste then the pax is your go to. If you like big clouds and decent flavor that you can do a whole session on then the mighty+ is what you want. If you want full convection that doesn’t waste flower, heats up instantly, taste amazing, all while hitting hard you want something like a Tinymight 2 or Roffu if you’re on a budget.

I smoked a lot of hash in the US in the early 2000s. Some of it was really good, but you either had to pay a lot for it ($25-40/g) or know a grower, and it was mainly used with flower in a joint or bowl, or in edibles. It was rarely used by itself (though hot knives were population) without flower, so it was seen as a novelty additive more than anything. Then butane hash oil started getting big around 05, and we switched to other concentrates. Hash is still a thing though, particularly bubble hash. Its mostly made and smoked by older generations. A dispensary near me sometimes carries it, but more often it's pressed into rosin (which is very expensive). Dry sift is pretty popular too, which is essentially unpressed hash. It is a different high, though, as the pressing and oxidizing of real hash mellows out the high in a relaxing way. So most people that want hash can make it at home. The black market is full of cheap, potent bud and concentrates, so hash just don't really fit in, demand wise.

My experience show us that everyone differs however we know from a decade of feedback that CBG seems to be a popular solution to number one on your list Sativa dominant products whether they are isolated , broad spectrum or full spectrum might be a good place to start with regards to number two on your list we think it’s mainly about the terpene set when it comes to uplifting the mood / energizing the mind. There are products on the market which include active ingredients aimed at sleep - which may or may not induce or help sleep however, we just advise taking CBD oil regularly at medium dose for two weeks to see if it helps you sleep … lots of customers feedback indicates full spectrum or broad spectrum with some minor cannabinoids can be more effective, but in the UK, these products are limited… and if Cbd is going to help you sleep then you will probably be able to tell after the regular use of any high-quality CBD product. If the product is labeled or advertised as broad spectrum you would expect to see a measurable amount of CBG or minor cannabinoid within the formulation… not just a milligram or two! So check the lab results before you buy. Loads of products are called these names but really they are just cbd isolate which all has a trace of CBDv - it’s not broad spectrum, it’s pulling the wool over your eyes. A truly broad spectrum product is either derived from its original broad spectrum extract or formulated to be truly broad spectrum product If you’re not sure, the best place to start is probably a broad spectrum product … one which contains CBG, which you can actually read the level on the product certificate of analysis really everyone differs I hope you find what you’re looking for Cbd - equals cbd only products which don’t contain more than trace cbd variants (cbdv, cbdA, etc). Most isolates used today all contain these traces - this does not make the end product broad spectrum. Broad spectrum cbd = cbd which also contains other cannabinoids above 0.2% in my book anyway! And has terpenes which are either added to the product or present in the original hemp extract. Full spectrum = as above with trace THC at a legal limit - you may find full spectrum products also contain more crude oil so flavonoids and other waxes not commonly found in refined extracts or isolates. My experience : 12 years in the field But we all have our own opinions I believe because we all react differently to Cannabinoid ratios and products - it’s natural due to the individual makeup of our EndoCannabinoid systems. Even then, the high you'd get overdosing on a CBD product should be fairly mild, and certainly nothing like the trip you went on. Can you order from stores in the U.S.? When you’re taking any cannabinoid orally you need to pair it with fat. So at the very least you should be combining the isolate with MCT oil or another carrier. But even then, if I take 100mg of CBD oil (whether isolate or full spectrum) on an empty stomach, I get much less effect than when I take it with a high fat meal (like the Chinese food I had last night). So the fat in the carrier oil helps in metabolizing it, and the more additional fat helps even more. I see you’re not someone who uses THC, like myself, but there’s a reason that people eating pot brownies became a thing. In addition to the heating process itself, called decarboxylation, it’s the high fat content of brownies that have made them a cliche when talking about edibles. Cannabinoids are fat soluble. When your intestines absorb the fat you get to experience the cannabinoids. Try 100mg with the fattiest meal you can stand to see the difference lol. Additionally, there are water soluble versions of CBD and THC made using a “nano encapsulation process” (whatever that means) and you just put that in a drink, no need for fat. I got a low priced powdered version of this from Gilded Extracts that I use daily, in addition to taking the more common fat soluble oil with my breakfast. The water soluble version works in like 20min and has a more pronounced and obvious effect. And since you mention sleep, the best choice for that is CBN (also needs the fat and best when combined with CBD and a little THC). But what you have WILL work for you. I’ve purchased from Zero Point before so I know they’re legit. I’ve put isolate on ice cream before. Just force yourself to eat some Ben & Jerry’s and it will all work out lol.

Oh wow no caffeine that's very cool. The best way to use CBD is: 1. Take it daily, and try to take the same amount every day. Consistency makes it work better. You won't ruin anything though if something happens on a day that causes you to end up needing some more after dealing with that thing (like say this "something" ruined your day and your earlier dose is definitely not cutting it, even a couple of hours after the "something" thing is over with). Just don't make a habit of it because then you won't be taking it consistently. 2. To find out how much you need to take, start with a low dose and slowly increase until you find your optimal dose (I guess give each new level a few days to see how it goes so that you get a miniature consistent dosing experience with the new level). You can keep going beyond that dose if you want though just to see if perhaps you still need more. When you reach a point where you're good and you don't need any more help from CBD, you can keep that as your daily dose. There are people who are finding very noticeable benefits from just a few milligrams and find very noticeable differences when they adjust their dose by a milligram or two, but then of course the range goes all the way to the people who need a few hundred milligrams in each dose, some have to do that multiple times per day! The only way to know how much you need is by experimenting. You're basically looking for your daily maintenance dose which is where you're not taking more than you need. 3. When you take some, wait 15 to 120 minutes to judge the effects of how much you took and remember that how this dose is affecting you the first time might be very different to how it's affecting you in a few days. Sometimes you can experience an illusion it's not working as well as the first time since now you're more used to it. 4. Always shake the bottle well before putting any into the dropper. 5. Don't touch the dropper with your tongue, but find a way to get your dose under your tongue (I use a spoon) and leave it there for a minimum of about 30 seconds, but ideally longer like for a few minutes. I like to do 5 minutes since it's a nice round number. This helps get the beneficial compounds that are floating around in the MCT carrier oil into your bloodstream faster. So you don't have to put it under your tongue. Not putting it under your tongue slightly lengthens the amount of time it takes to begin feeling the benefits. I think I'm all out of tips. So, 5 tips it is! I like that. It's a nice round number. Anyway, by the time you finish reading this, I'll have gone to bed. Generally you will always get better results from something that has all the cannabinoids and terpenes in rather than a distillate or isolate. I assumed there would of been a detectable amount of THC in your buds TBH as I've never once had any that have actually had 0%/non detectable, just levels below 0.2% are allowed in the UK, and the limit I think is higher in USA (0.3% maybe?), and IIRC they also allow high THCa which will convert to THC when decarbed/heated. Personally I make my carts/oil with distillates, broad spectrum distillates, and sometimes added isolates, but the final mixture will have more cannabinoids in much higher percentages than even flower, so it's basically all the components dissected and put back together. It's hard for me to explain or give an example with CBD as it's not psychoactive, but if for example you make a cart with just THC distillate in it, it will get you high and be strong, but if you add loads of CBD and other cannabinoids, as well as terpenes etc.. even though the final mix has less THC than before, like half of what it would be with straight distillate, the high is better, more rounded, and lasts a lot longer. It's a strange thing, because if you did a blind test you would assume the mixed cart has more THC due to the better and longer lasting high. Entourage effect basically, but it's very hard to notice when THC is not in the mix, at least for me anyway. Personally I don't find CBD very useful at all on it's own, or even mixed in a broad spectrum oil/cart. CBN I always have a good 3-5% in my mixes and notice that a lot, it makes me nice and sleepy, but again that is easy to notice because it is psychoactive unlike the majority of the other cannabinoids.

Yeah I'd say you did. Edibles in general are a fucking scam these days. I was getting the gummy's for a while because people like them but they're nowhere near the potency they claim and the effect is very one dimensional because they use distillate which is just basically pure THC. I'm old-school, I got into the game making brownies back in the day and I recently returned to my roots. It took a little nudging to catch on because everybody's brother made a shitty batch of brownies that didn't do shit once so people don't trust homemade edibles but I make mine right. I make my own extracts at home with a magic budder maker, which means full spectrum extracts. I'm not just spraying on some THC distillate or melting in some crappy wax that I couldn't sell, you are getting everything that could possibly survive the process, all of the cannabinoids and addition amount of turpines as well. Turpines won't survive the baking or cooking process so that's not as important, but you could also make a tincture and take that directly where those turpines may come into play for their different medical uses. The effect is noticeably different, it is more of a psychedelic experience in a way, the body high is more euphoric and you get the giggles like we did back in the day when we first started smoking. If you have access to Bud I would highly recommend looking into making homemade extracts. You don't have to use a machine like mine and you don't have to use butter or even oil, which are considerably more touchy than my preferred method, the Everclear method. A lot of the failed brownie batches back in the day were because somebody burned the butter or didn't simmer the oil long enough or fucked up straining it and lost a lot of product. With the Everclear method there is no cooking required unless you have my machine. You simply chop up and decarboxylate your bud and then you add Everclear to it and put it in a cool dark place for a week or two. Give it a good shake every once in a while And when it seems saturated strain out the plant matter. The only difference in what I do is the machine heats and agitates it over an eight hour period to speed the process up. You can allow excess alcohol to evaporate or add more to your desired strength, after that you can add it to pretty much any baked good or alcoholic drink. There is another method though, where I got my start all those years ago. Take your bud, decarboxylate, grind that shit into a powder and just throw it in the mix. The texture might be a little bit off and some people might complain but you'll be lit. The most important part is proper decarboxylation, I do 240° for 45 minutes. My one warning for this method is if you use really high-grade bud for this there is a chance you might black out. I know that sounds ridiculous but It happened to multiple people multiple times

I like to make a very refined MCT coconut oil for candy making and drinks where I use top quality flower and dont press down on the mesh or grind it up too fine. Just kind of gravity filter it through a cheesecloth it for a few seconds. Then toss the remaining oil soaked mulch into a cannabutter batch filled with trim and larf, you can even toss in moldy or mildewy weed if it isn't too bad lol. Grind it to dust, squeeze the life out of the mulch, then filter the butter out with water a bunch of times You can use a paper towel if you dont have a cheesecloth but be careful not to cook it on high heat as high heat will degrade the THC.

So, eating a THC bath bomb sounds pretty wild and creative, but I wouldn't recommend it. Those bath bombs are made for external use only, and they often contain essential oils and other ingredients that aren't meant to be ingested. Eating them could lead to stomach upset or worse. Even though they’re cheap and packed with THC, the other ingredients are not designed for digestion. The THC in them is also likely formulated for topical absorption, not for effective ingestion, so you might not even get the effects you’re looking for if you ate one. Totally get the thriftiness though. A cool and safe alternative, check out those THC syringes, do know which one I'm talking about—they're super potent and you can mix them into foods. For example, you could blend a bit into a small jar of peanut butter. It mixes well and you can control the dosage easily. Plus, it's a tasty way to enjoy the effects!

I knew dogs weren't supposed to have any, so I was expecting her to maybe get a little sick from it or go into a panic, but she just sat there just as I would - super calm and red-eyed with a shitfaced grin. For the next year, I kept feeding her THC/CBD peanut butter sandwiches and it seemed to do wonders for her. I think she started to recognize it too, because she would start to give me signs she wants some when her breathing would get bad. She started hounding me anytime I would start rolling. It got to a point where even my buddies were telling me my dog was a fiend. She ended up living another year after the really bad night, at which point things got bad enough we had to put her down. I love having conversations about stuff, I just share what I’ve learned and don’t want other people to get duped into spending more of their hard earned money on overpriced stuff like I used to do. Especially people going through WDs from stuff. Your mental state is everything. You can make your own D8 tinctures for about $2 each, 60 ml (60 doses) tinctures, pretty much liquid weed edibles. The vendor I use has 30 gram jars of 95% D8 distillate, the remaining percentage is CBN and other cannabinoids Distillate is a little more tricky to handle than Isolate because it’s so sticky, and there are a couple extra steps involved in making a tincture from Distillates. The differences are - - You have to keep the distillate at room temp, so it’s not too sticky/gooey, or too hard from being too cold or else it’ll shatter all over the place. Use something hard like 1 scissor blade to get it out of the container. Use a toothpick or another pair is scissors to get it off the original scissors. - Cut a 2x2 inch square of wax paper, and put that on your scale. The distillate comes off of that perfectly. Make sure to TARE the wax paper first lol - After you weigh the distillate and it’s on the wax paper, stick it in the freezer for 2 minutes so it hardens up. - Use your fingers to pull it off and plop em into the em into the empty tincture bottle - Pour in the MCT oil - Give it a hot water bath, to melt the Distillate into the oil, by Boiling some water, and pour the pre-boiled water into a regular ceramic Coffee cup - Put the almost finished tincture bottle into the boiled water cup, let it sit a couple minutes, pull it out and shake it up really hard, put it back in there, repeat u til you don’t see any distillate chunks floating around. If it’s not melted yet by the time the water isn’t that hot, just pour more boiled water into the cup so it’s hot again. After a couple times, it’ll be fully dissolved and ready to use. Sometimes I have to re-fill the cup with boiled water if I put large Distillate chunks in there. That’s it

I just kept going up, and up, and up, and up, higher and higher with no end in sight. I remember looking at my buddy’s eyes and internally panicking because his eyes were so red I was convinced he was bleeding out of his eyes. I had genuinely never seen that shade of red in someone’s eyes before but I still had a tiny bit of logic left in my monkey brain to remember that weed does that. We were staying in my parents’ camper in my backyard and at this point my parents didn’t know I partook. We scrambled in our cannabis induced stupor to shut off all the lights in the camper and make it look like we were asleep and as I lay in bed for what felt like hours waiting for my mom to come into the camper (she never did) I sent myself into a panic thinking about how much trouble I would be in if she knew and how disappointed she would be in me because she hated weed at the time due to my dead beat druggie uncles. My heart rate was beating faster than a formula 1 car’s engines and I couldn’t breathe and my stomach was twisting in knots. They’re amazing! But even better if you make them yourself and a million times cheaper! Bit fkn messy but you need, One large box of nerds - the rainbow ones are my fave Whatever haribo gummies you like the taste of (haribo melts the best) - melt your gummies in a double boiler on low, you can add a table spoon of water to help them reach a nice consistency. You want it to be smooth and when you lift the spoon from the bowl and drizzle the mixture back in, it should slightly hold its shape on the surface before sinking into the mix within 5-8 seconds - let your gummy mix sit for a few minutes (sometimes takes up to 10 to cool enough) before dumping the whole large pack of nerds in that bad boy. Mix it together with the spoon and pop down some baking paper onto your working bench. - to roll the gummy nerds mix without it sticking to everything you own, wet down your hands and shake off excess water for like 1 second so it’s not dripping, pick up teaspoon size amounts and roll them in your palm until they form a rough ball. Put them onto the baking paper and let them cool for 1-2hrs before eating That recipe makes a great amount for under $10 and they’re nerds all the way through encased in gummy. They can also be made into edibles but I eat a shit tonne so I leave them as is. Happy snacking y’all

I can help. Legal: The Compassionate Use act allows for patients to access completely legal THC products. The dispensary options are extremely limited and difficult to access. Flower is still illegal, so you're limited to tinctures and gummies, and boy are they expensive.... To get on the program you can go through any number of prescription services online. I used veriheal, and it cost about 150-200 per year. Because of the Texas farm bill, things get complicated quickly. In short, delta-9 THC products are kinda legal. It has to do with the THC:Weight ratio. A tiny gummy with 10mg of THC is illegal, but a big one isn't due to weight. A company called sweet sensei makes amazing gummies and chocolates, you can find these at higher end smoke shops, or anywhere in Austin. Delta 8, THC-A, THC-H, and THC-O are pretty much fair game, but the quality is super sketchy. Any flower products seem to be shitty and low THC. TECHNICALLY, THC-A is a non-oxidized version of normal weed, which becomes normal THC when smoked, but it's garbage compared to good weed. Certain cities, such as houston and Austin have decriminalized weed, meaning you can have certain amounts before it becomes an actual charge. That said, state police don't give a fuck about city law and will fuck you dry for pretty much any quantity. In Houston you can have like up to 5 ounces before it becomes a big deal. Illegal: ANY unregulated delta-9 products are illegal. If you get pulled over with your prescription you're good, police can call the registry to confirm you're on the list. If you have a bag of black market shit, you're going to face some sort of consequences based on local or state law. Some cities give you a fine, others will charge you with possession or worse. Again, if you have any amount over the local limit you're in deep shit. Check your local laws. Might be forgetting something, let me know if this doesn't answer your question. Edibles made on a commercial scale are tricky. When you're making batches that big it's hard to get the THC to homogenize throughout the entire batch, so you get some parts that are super light doses and some parts that are mega doses. This is why you see stories all over the place talking about how weak or way too strong their edibles were. I recommend making your own so you can control the dose more easily. Infuse some coconut oil and make brownies with it, you'll never look back.

Start low and slow. You can always eat another gummy or smoke more, but you can’take your body process it any faster For smoking joints/blunts: try to draw the smoke into your mouth first before inhaling. This helps cool the smoke a bit so you won’t cough as much. Take a drag or two and pass to the next person. If you don’t feel anything by the time it gets back to you, repeat until you’re comfortable. Remember it’s like alcohol: once you stop, you’ll continue to feel the effects for awhile afterwards. And it’ll take a bit after you stop for it to peak in your system. So stop just short of “hoo boy I’m stoned” to avoid getting nauseous. For bongs/pipes: these are typically inhaled directly into the lungs. Breathe in steadily but gently. And stop drawing smoke when you’re about 3/4 through, then either lift the bowl of your bong or remove your finger from the carb hole of a pipe and draw until you clear the smoke and breathe fresh air. Then exhale steadily. Bongs will get you higher, faster since you smoke more at once/take larger hits. Same with pipes (at least for me). Take a single hit and pass. And like with joints, just wait and see between each hit so you don’t overdo it For vapes and e-nails: also inhaled directly to lungs. A bit smoother than dry herb smoking, and easier to bring to a party. Pens and rigs differ, so ask how it works if you’re not sure. No one is gonna make fun of you for not knowing. Vapes feel different than a smoke high. And dabs are VERY strong, and I can’t recommend that for your first go-round. Wait until you’re more comfortable with how weed affects you before you try concentrates For edibles: less is often more, especially with low tolerances. Most gummies and candies from dispos are either 5 or 10mg. Always ask the dosage, and don’t be afraid to ask for a half or even a quarter to start with. Edibles often take longer to hit than smoking/vaping as it has to be digested first. Start very small, and wait at least ONE HOUR before you have more. If you go for another piece, wait another 30-45 minutes. Edibles can take up to 90 minutes to hit you. And they hit harder for longer. They also give more of a body high than head feeling, so if you don’t like not feeling in control of your body if and when the couch lock hits, remember that you’re just high, nothing is wrong. And your friends, if they’re good people, aren’t going to let anything happen to you. Remember to eat a little something before you get high to combat the munchies, and drink lots of water to help with dry mouth (or eat a piece of regular hard candy). Have all of your snacks and drinks and entertainment at-hand before you start so you’re not tempted to get in the car. Stoned driving is just as dangerous as drunk driving and your French fries aren’t worth it If you get too high and start to feel nauseous, touch an ice cube to your eyelids lids and cheeks. Idk why it works, but it almost instantly helps get rid of the pukey feeling and spins. Naps are also a good way to ride out the worst of an intense high. Just get comfy somewhere quiet, and remember that you’re not actually dying, you’re just high. And to-date, no one has died from weed, and you’re unlikely to be the first Never be afraid to speak up if you have a question or are suspicious of whatever form of weed you’re using (the color-sprayed flower is one to avoid like the plague). If your friends are good eggs, they’ll understand. And remember that it’s ok if you decide that weed isn’t for you or that you don’t like how it makes you feel, and it’s ok to back out after you get there if you change your mind You can still hang out and chill, you’ll just be the sober friend who then has to make sure no one spends their rent money on door dashing crumbl cookies at 2am

I’ve never thought about it really, but I think I actually do? It’s not at all exciting, but it is real. This usually takes 2-4 hours and I would say this happens 90% of the time. I sit on the bathroom floor with the fan on and a candle lit. I use a bong with a shit ton of ice. Listen to my favorite music while it kicks in. If you want a feel for the vibe, listen to Be Sweet by Japanese Breakfast. I get up shortly after before the full effects take hold. Then I actually take a few minutes to clean up and leave no trace. For some reason that part is satisfying to me - like it’s a secret that I only want to know. I grab my headphones that I leave in a kitchen drawer and go to my back porch. I sit there for at least 10-20 minutes just listening to my favorites. I know that it’s kicked in because my mind starts to pick apart lyrics and relate it deeply to my life. That’s when it’s time to go inside. I grab my favorite cup and fill it to the brim with ice and water. I grab my favorite snack - salt and vinegar almonds. I go to my bedroom. I lay down with my headphones still in. Windows open so the sun shines on my face. Now it’s fully kicked in and I’m having really hazy deep thoughts. Sometimes I stop and write them down into my notes app. Most times, I turn on my favorite show right now, don’t laugh, Gilmore Girls. Something about them living a normal life feels comforting to me. I watch for an episode or two until the horniness kicks in. It always kicks in and it’s always amazing. Headphones still in too. Afterwards, I chill for a little with my music back on, but this time no headphones. I do that for a while until I finally get up. I make a bath. Eucalyptus mint bubble bath and the lights off. Sometimes a candle. I just lay in the water with Gilmore Girls still on in the background. I just float for an hour or two with my thoughts and my sour grape vape. It’s my bathtub vape lol. Dry myself off and get back into bed. I don’t get dressed because one of my favorite feelings is cold clean skin on a toasty bed. I just cuddle up with my snack and watch a list of content that I really love. There’s a peak into my life! And what I will be doing tonight lol

I've been on CBD for 10+ years now and have certainly felt what you describe from oils. I gave up oils all together because of this. I found it was too unpredictable. Sometimes, I would feel very little, other times I would be out of it (incredibly drowsy/lethargic). My doctor said it could be due to the carrier oil (most use MCT) and apparently this can cause it to enter your blood stream too quickly and can cause a dip in blood pressure. I primarily just vape CBD flower now and find the effects to be more predictable and more favorable. Also want to add that in my experience, CBD feels like an adaptogen. If you're tired and take CBD, it will make you feel more tired. If you're wired and take CBD, it may cause you to feel more alert. I've learned that I can't take CBD too early in the morning or too close to bed. Mid day works best for my needs. I recommend playing around with dosage and keeping a journal listing your overall mood, if you were tired, what you ate, your dose, etc. May see a pattern that way.

No, bad idea. 4.6mg of THC in gel capsule. You think that whole capsule is 4.6mg? It's not. It's 4.6mg of THC mixed with a carrier oil. I am someone who started smoking (again) consistently about a year ago after not smoking for most of my 20s because of how paranoid weed made me. I can’t tell you what to do but I can tell you I’m glad I tried again. Some takeaways: 1. THC is a vasoconstrictor. If you live in a cold climate or tend to get cold easily this is something to keep in mind. Feeling cold would sometimes trigger my paranoia and make me feel very self-conscious about my body. If you’re taking them sublingually the idea is you’re allowing it to fully absorb through your oral mucosa which helps bypass the liver and its first pass metabolism. This allows quicker onset of effects, but by bypassing the liver you’re also not allowing the cannabinoids to be broken down into the liver into various other breakdown metabolites many of which have their own effects and benefits. Like with THC for example, its main breakdown metabolites many in the liver is 11-hydroxy-THC which is many times more potent than D9 THC itself. This is why oral edibles feel different for most people vs inhaled cannabis. Some people lack these liver enzymes and thus tinctures are the next best thing as it bypasses the liver allowing the drugs to enter bloodstream directly. Now you’ll get a different ratio of cannabinoids and metabolites when taken sublingually vs orally. Sublingual will feel more akin to inhaled cannabis so either smoked or vaped. So if you do want to try tinctures make sure to allow the tincture to fully dissolve in your mouth for 5-15 minutes, try not to let the oil get diluted by saliva, as you want it to be as concentrated as possible for best absorption, and you want to try to not swallow any of it for at least 5 minutes, preferably 15minutes. One thing you can try to help with this is to place the oil between your lower cheek and gums, this is called buccal administration, but will absorb at a similar rate as under the tongue, but it has a lower chance of being accidentally swallowed or diluted by saliva. If that still doesn’t work, I’d suggest just taking the tincture orally since you said orally taking gummies had decent relief for you before. The tincture is already cannabinoids dissolved into oil, so you’ll just be able to swallow and should give you similar effect to the gummies if they’re similar in potency. Keep in mind there’s little to no regulation surrounding hemp, so there’s tons of products that say they’re X mg’s and in reality they’re either way more or way less. All depends on the brand and stuff how accurate this labeling will be, but it takes a lot of dialing in to make accurately dosed products all the time. Also if you’re using a full spectrum product then you’re getting a lot of your relief from the combination of low dose THC plus the CBD, along with a few other minor cannabinoids, along with a long list of trace cannabinoids, and potentially terpenes and flavanoids as well. So full spectrum is always the move for the best chance of relief. All that said, not all full spectrum is created equal, since the just contain whatever cannabinoids in the plant, one companies full spectrum could have 50mg CBD/2mg THC/4mg CBG per ml and another could have 50mg CBD/4mg THC/8mg CBG, which can make a huge difference as well. Whatever strain or mix of biomass they used will have a huge impact on the final product. So try the tincture again this time bucally for 15min, if still no relief then try swallowing the tincture after eating a light meal with a decent amount of fat. If you still have no relief then the tincture you got is either weaker then the gummies, or the flower they used to make them doesn’t help your condition as much as the flower used for the gummies. Hope this helps friend, take care!!

The realm of weed these days includes flower, concentrates, edibles, and topicals. I have no experience with topicals. Flower is obviously just the dried/cured flowers from the cannabis plant. This is great to smoke in bowls, bong, and joints. It can also be vaped several ways which is also great. Bongs work by filtering some of the water soluble particles out of the smoke, as well as making sure plant matter doesnt get into your mouth/throat/lungs like can happen with pipes and poorly rolled joints. Concentrates include dabbable things like carts, as well as BHO/Rosin/etc... Dabbing is heating up some kind of thing, usually quartz ceramic or metal, then putting a cannabis concentrate on it to vaporize it. Concentrates also includes things like bubble hash and dry sift hashes, as well as other things. These arent quite as 'dabbable,' probably don't mess with these for a while. They are a little bit more challenging to smoke. You can just put some on the top of your bowls or roll them into joints though. Edibles are edibles. There are a lot of pure THC edibles, as well as edibles made from more full spectrum cannabis plant extracts. The pure THC edibles are more stimulating in general, the more full spectrum ones are more like what you would expect. Start with like 5-10mg, but if you have a tolerance you will need 2-4x more at least. There is no best way to get high. Experiment with several different methods, and you will find some you strongly prefer. It is worth it to buy bongs, rolling papers, making edibles, buying edibles, buying an oil rig for dabs, etc.... You will find some methods you strongly prefer over time. You will also notice the high from each method/type of cannabis is slightly different. You might also find that you might smoke concentrates for a few months, then swap back to bong or joints for a while, then back, etc.. Maybe you find you really like edibles and mostly use those. I'm sure I left out some popular smoking methods, but none are really 'the best.' They pretty much all have pros/cons and slightly different effects. Vaping or dabs might be better if you need to be up and stimulated, where a joint or bong might be better if your just sitting and chilling.

Clogging happens with disposables and with regular vape carts, and most places that sell carts will also sell compatible batteries for them. You can also find them at places that sell cigarettes or nicotine vapes, because the cartridges are similar. They might be cheaper at those stores. I've read many different claims as to what causes them to clog and how to prevent it, from the cartridge quality to oil quality, temperature settings on batteries, how the vape is stored, etc. As for how to fix clogs once they happen, heating up the oil might make it less viscous to allow air through again. If your disposable/vape battery has a button, usually if you press it twice it will heat the oil prior to using and that can be helpful for some people too. Personally I've only had carts start to clog up once they reached pretty much no distillate left, but a common thing I've seen from my friends is their vapes leaking and clogging more if they were left in a hot car.

I'd like to add to the "always go with manufacturer guidance". I'm a CBD retailer (3rd party products), it'd be super easy for me to say "use as the bottle says" but I know you can get good response as a first time user from a minimal dose (we start everyone on 5mg dosage with a full-spectrum oil). The manufacturer wants you rinsing through your bottle and back in for more. You WILL build a tolerance to CBD over time with continued use as anything. Now it's a slow process, but you'll speed that up by jumping in with a high dose. You can always work up but not down! Some people do need a higher dose dependant on time, severity of ailment or just their bodies functioning. If the dose used has done the job for you, stick with that dose twice or even three times (if needed) a day. You will build a level of CBD in the body and the effects will build and stack over the following weeks/months. If after a few days, you find that dose isn't cutting the mustard in day time, up the dose slightly. CBD isn't cut-and-dry Q&A. I have customers that swear by a particular oil whilst others don't like oils at all and prefer capsules, vapes or patches etc. Some customers use it once a day, some three times. I used to be twice a day (anxiety myself) and now I don't even use it everyday! It's very much a suck it and see thing when it comes to CBD from method of administration, dosage and frequency of doses. If you want to understand how CBD works with the body, research "The Endocannabinoid System". Add " and xxx (any ailment)" to the end of that (GAD in your case) and it will pull up ample research, articles and case studies specifically regarding that. The Endocannabinoid System is the largest system in the body that regulates almost every action within the body. There's alot to take in when it comes to CBD, if you're after guidance or any information, give me a shout by all means and I'll help where possible. It's the whole reason I've got the shop! But stick with it and you'll soon have a love and appreciation for CBD as all of us do, have a gooden
